source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A beam of light, with $\lambda=5000 \AA$, traveling in the $z$-direction is polarised at $45^{\circ}$ to the $x$-direction. It passes through a Kerr cell, i.e., a substance such that $n_{x}-n_{y}=K E^{2}$, where $n_{x}$ and $n_{y}$ are the refractive indices for light polarized in the $x$ and $y$ directions. $E$ is the strength of an externally applied electric field in the $x$-direction. The cell has length 1 cm and $K=2.5 \times 10^{-6}$ (meter) $^{2} /(\text { volts })^{2}$. If the light, after passing through the Kerr cell, passes through a polarization analyzer whose plane of polarisation is perpendicular to that of the original beam, calculate the smallest value of $E$ which gives maximum transmission. Assume the effect of the electric field on reflection at the Kerr cell is negligible.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A beam of light, with $\lambda=5000 \AA$, traveling in the $z$-direction is polarised at $45^{\circ}$ to the $x$-direction. It passes through a Kerr cell, i.e., a substance such that $n_{x}-n_{y}=K E^{2}$, where $n_{x}$ and $n_{y}$ are the refractive indices for light polarized in the $x$ and $y$ directions. $E$ is the strength of an externally applied electric field in the $x$-direction. The cell has length 1 cm and $K=2.5 \times 10^{-6}$ (meter) $^{2} /(\text { volts })^{2}$. If the light, after passing through the Kerr cell, passes through a polarization analyzer whose plane of polarisation is perpendicular to that of the original beam, calculate the smallest value of $E$ which gives maximum transmission. Assume the effect of the electric field on reflection at the Kerr cell is negligible.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images field contains a PIL image object string, not a file path or URL
force_requires_image set from record structure including vision_relevance="essential" and image caption, not from explicit wording in the question
metadata.reasoning_chain:
The Kerr cell induces birefringence. As the direction of the electric field is $45^\circ$ to the direction of polarization of the light, the amplitudes of the components polarized parallel to the $x$ and $y$ directions are the same. When the phase difference $\delta$ between the components is
$$
\delta = \frac{2\pi l(n_{x}-n_{y})}{\lambda} = \frac{2\pi l K E^{2}}{\lambda} = (2j+1)\pi, \quad j=0 \text{ or integer},
$$
the Kerr cell acts as a half-wave plate and rotates the plane of polarization by $90^\circ$ so that it coincides with the transmission axis of the analyzer and gives maximum transmission. Putting $j=0$ yields the smallest $E$, $E_{\min}$, which is given by
$$
E_{\min}^{2} = \frac{\lambda}{2 K l} = 10~\mathrm{V}^{2}/\mathrm{m}^{2} \text{ for } l=1~\mathrm{cm}.
$$
